operations.trigger: User has <= 10 labeled examples and a metric
operations.action: Apply dspy.teleprompt.BootstrapFewShot(metric=m, max_bootstrapped_demos=4, max_rounds=1)

operations.trigger: User has 30-50 labeled examples
operations.action: Apply BootstrapFewShotWithRandomSearch for best-of-N candidate search
operations.output: Best candidate program over multiple bootstrap rounds

operations.trigger: User has 200+ labeled examples and compute budget
operations.action: Apply MIPROv2(metric=m, auto='light'); escalate to medium only if light gives >5% lift
operations.output: Jointly-optimized instructions + few-shot demos via Bayesian optimization

operations.trigger: User can express textual error feedback (test diffs, schema violations, judge rationales)
operations.action: Apply dspy.GEPA with a metric that returns dspy.Prediction(score=..., feedback=...)
operations.output: Reflection-evolved prompts; sample-efficient (10 examples + 20-100 evals can yield improvement)

operations.trigger: Need zero-shot prompts in production output (no demos)
operations.action: MIPROv2 with max_bootstrapped_demos=0, max_labeled_demos=0
operations.output: Instruction-only optimization; final prompt has no few-shot examples

operations.trigger: User switches LM family (e.g. GPT-4o -> Llama-3-8B)
operations.action: Re-compile the program with the new LM configured; do not migrate the old program.json
operations.output: New compiled artifact (program.llama8b.json) tuned for the smaller model's quirks

operations.trigger: Have a compiled program on a strong model; need cheaper production inference
operations.action: Chain MIPROv2 -> BootstrapFinetune(student=small_lm, teacher=optimized)
operations.output: Finetuned weights on a 1B-7B student model retaining most of the teacher's quality

operations.trigger: Open-ended generation task with no exact-match metric
operations.action: Decompose into sub-judges (factual? on-topic? concise? non-hedging?) + explicit length penalty; return bool when trace is not None, float otherwise
operations.output: Robust multi-criteria metric suitable for compilation

operations.trigger: Budget-constrained compile
operations.action: Use cheap optimizer LM (gpt-4o-mini) as prompt_model even when task_model is expensive (gpt-4o)
operations.output: Comparable optimization quality at fraction of cost

operations.trigger: Before any MIPROv2 run
operations.action: Always start with auto='light' (~$2). Never start at 'heavy'.
operations.output: Cheap signal on whether the optimizer is the bottleneck

operations.trigger: Setting up train/val for any prompt-based optimizer (not GEPA)
operations.action: Use unusual 20% train / 80% val split to prevent overfit

operations.trigger: Setting up train/val for GEPA
operations.action: Use standard ML split (maximize train; val just large enough to match test distribution)

operations.trigger: New DSPy program
operations.action: Default module choice = dspy.ChainOfThought(Sig)
operations.output: Baseline with built-in step-by-step reasoning field

operations.trigger: Task requires external tools (search, calculator, API)
operations.action: Use dspy.ReAct(Sig, tools=[...])

operations.trigger: Math, parsing, deterministic computation needed
operations.action: Use dspy.ProgramOfThought(Sig) - LM emits code, code is executed

operations.trigger: Ship a compiled DSPy program to production
operations.action: Use compiled.save('./v1/', save_program=True) for whole-program save (includes dep versions); deploy via FastAPI(dspy.asyncify(program)) or mlflow.dspy.log_model(program, task='llm/v1/chat')

operations.trigger: Serverless / AWS Lambda deployment
operations.action: Disable cache via dspy.LM(cache=False); set DSP_CACHEDIR and DSPY_CACHEDIR to /tmp

operations.trigger: Compile hangs / single trial stuck > 30 min
operations.action: Abort; reduce max_bootstrapped_demos and max_labeled_demos (defaults: 4); reduce minibatch_size (default 35 -> try 16); reduce num_trials

operations.trigger: Need to inspect what the compiled program is sending to the LM
operations.action: Call dspy.inspect_history(n=3) to see the last N LM invocations with full prompts
operations.output: Prompt + response transcripts for the most recent calls

operations.trigger: Need to enforce hard output constraints (development time)
operations.action: Wrap module output with dspy.Assert(condition, msg); halts after max retries with AssertionError

operations.trigger: Need to encourage but not enforce output constraints (evaluation time)
operations.action: Use dspy.Suggest(condition, msg); logs failures and continues execution

operations.trigger: Have a LangGraph workflow with LLM-calling nodes
operations.action: Inside each node body, load and invoke a compiled DSPy program; LangGraph handles state/routing, DSPy handles prompt quality

operations.trigger: Have a LlamaIndex retriever and a generator step
operations.action: Wrap the generator as dspy.Predict(context, question -> answer); compile against retrieval + answer metrics separately

operations.trigger: User wants to compile but has no metric
operations.action: Refuse to compile. Help them write a metric first (exact match, sub-judge decomposition, or text feedback)
operations.output: Either a metric or a re-routed task that doesn't need DSPy

operations.trigger: User wants to start with auto='heavy'
operations.action: Push back; require auto='light' as a probe first
operations.output: Cheap signal informs whether escalation is worth it

operations.trigger: Optimization plateaus across multiple optimizers
operations.action: Return to Stage 1 (program structure) or Stage 2 (metric design). Do NOT keep trying new optimizers.

operations.trigger: Drafting a Signature
operations.action: Use semantically meaningful field names ('question' not 'input1'); promote to class-based Signature when types/desc/Literal constraints matter

operations.trigger: Multiple LMs in production rotation
operations.action: Maintain one compiled artifact per (program × LM) pair; e.g. program.gpt4o.json and program.llama8b.json; A/B test

operations.trigger: Decide if optimization is feasible
operations.action: Floor = 30 examples (minimal value); recommended = 300; required for MIPROv2 to avoid overfit = 200
operations.output: Go/no-go on optimization based on dataset size

operations.trigger: Writing a metric that will be used for both compile and eval
operations.action: Inside the metric: return strict bool when trace is not None (compile mode); return float otherwise (eval mode)

research_caveats: Discord war stories were paraphrased from public write-ups; raw Discord transcripts were not directly fetched (private channel).
DSPy version referenced: v3.2.1 per GitHub README at research time. Minor API drift in 3.x is possible.
GEPA is recent (ICLR 2026 oral, published Jul 2025); practitioner case studies are thinner than for MIPROv2.
Cost figures ($2-$3 per typical light compile) are from official FAQ; actual costs vary with LM choice and dataset size.
One initial WebFetch to dspy.ai/ failed (socket closed); subsequent fetches succeeded — content cross-referenced from multiple sources.
